name: González Lacasa Reservoir - El Rasillo Loop
name_original: Embalse de González Lacasa, El Rasillo, Peñaloscintos – circular
description: Jog a moderate 7.8-mile (12.5 km) circular route around Embalse de González Lacasa, offering scenic reservoir and mountain views.
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse González-Lacasa offers a compelling natural setting for outdoor activities, situated at approximately 1,000 meters altitude in the Camero Nuevo region of La Rioja, Spain. The reservoir is enveloped by dense pine forests, providing shaded routes and a refreshing environment for running. Fed by the Iregua River, the area features deep waters and mountainous terrain, contributing to a varied landscape for runners. The perimeter of the reservoir is about 4.3 miles (7 km), offering a substantial distance for running routes. Best jogging routes around Embalse González-Lacasa The most popular jogging route is González Lacasa Reservoir - El Rasillo Loop, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding pine forests. Another top favourite among local runners is Olmo de El Rasillo – Peña Hincada Pass loop from El Rasillo de Cameros, a difficult 19.7 miles (31.7 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and expansive mountain vistas. Local runners also love the Olmo de El Rasillo – El Rasillo de Cameros loop from El Rasillo de Cameros, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through dense pine forests and along the reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Jogging around Embalse González-Lacasa is defined by extensive pine forests, the serene reservoir, and varied mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer runs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse González-Lacasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: There are 6 dedicated running routes detailed in this guide for Embalse González-Lacasa. These routes offer a variety of distances and difficulties, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, while there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this guide, there are 2 moderate routes that are suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38144908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rasillo de Cameros – Olmo de El Rasillo loop from Ortigosa de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.1 km (4.4 miles) path with manageable elevation, offering a pleasant experience for beginners or those seeking a relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: The running routes around Embalse González-Lacasa are renowned for their stunning natural beauty. You'll primarily experience dense pine forests, offering shaded paths and a refreshing atmosphere. Many trails also provide breathtaking views of the deep, cold waters of the reservoir itself, and the surrounding mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Cameros.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around the reservoir?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990082907/gonzalez-lacasa-reservoir-el-rasillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir - El Rasillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 12.5 km (7.8 miles) long and provides scenic views of the reservoir and its surrounding pine forests. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo – El Rasillo de Cameros loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, covering 18.0 km (11.2 miles).

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Yes, the Embalse González-Lacasa area is generally dog-friendly. Many trails winding through the pine forests and alongside the reservoir are suitable for running with your canine companion. There's even a specific bathing area for dogs at the reservoir during summer, making it a great destination for active pets.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running at Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: The area is particularly pleasant for running during the warmer months, thanks to its altitude of approximately 1,000 meters which provides cooler temperatures and pleasant summer nights. The dense pine forests offer ample shade, making it comfortable even on sunnier days. Spring and autumn also provide ideal conditions with mild weather and vibrant natural colors.

Q: Are there facilities like cafes or restaurants near the running routes?
A: Yes, the Club Náutico El Rasillo, located directly on the reservoir, features a cafeteria and restaurant with outdoor terraces that offer excellent views. This is a great spot to refuel or relax after your run. Additionally, the nearby villages like El Rasillo de Cameros would offer further options.

Q: Is parking available for runners at Embalse González-Lacasa?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in the vicinity of the reservoir, especially near popular access points and the Club Náutico El Rasillo. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes for the most convenient parking options.

Q: What are the longest and most challenging running routes in the area?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo – Peña Hincada Pass loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.7 km (19.7 miles) path with significant elevation gain, offering expansive mountain vistas. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rasillo de Cameros – Olmo de El Rasillo loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 14.0 km (8.7 miles) route with substantial climbing.

Q: What other activities can I enjoy at Embalse González-Lacasa besides jogging?
A: Embalse González-Lacasa is a hub for outdoor activities. You can enjoy various water sports at the Club Náutico El Rasillo, including canoeing, sailing, windsurfing, and pedal boating. There are also designated swimming areas with amenities during summer, fishing opportunities, and numerous trails for hiking and cycling. It's a perfect spot for a full day of outdoor recreation.

Q: Are the running paths well-maintained and easy to follow?
A: The area offers a mix of well-maintained paths and more natural trails. The 'Senda verde' connecting El Rasillo to the Nautical Club is a dedicated pedestrian path. Generally, routes around the reservoir and through the pine forests are well-trodden, but using a navigation app like komoot is always recommended to stay on track, especially on longer or more remote trails.

Q: What makes Embalse González-Lacasa a unique place for running?
A: Its unique appeal lies in the combination of its high altitude (around 1,000 meters), the refreshing presence of the large reservoir, and the extensive, dense pine forests. This creates a distinct microclimate and a serene, picturesque environment that is ideal for invigorating runs, offering both shaded paths and open views of water and mountains.
tips: Better known by local people as “El swamp del Rasillo” or “El Pantano de Ortigosa”, it is located at an altitude of 1,000 meters, surrounded by pine forests, in the Sierra de Cameros in La Rioja. There are good views, and the possibility of bathing, sunbathing, eating and having a drink.

El Rasillo is a town in Los Cameros that has a livestock tradition. Today it is a tourist town located between the swamp and the mountain.

A very special corner that deserves a visit on wheels.
Just a dozen houses, on a hillside and surrounded by nature. It has its charm.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de González Lacasa, El Rasillo, Peñaloscintos circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the González Lacasa Reservoir, particularly around the nautical club area in El Rasillo de Cameros. This area offers public access and facilities, making it a convenient starting point for the circular route.

faqs.question: Is this jogging trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users. There are no explicit restrictions mentioned, but responsible pet ownership is encouraged.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail is located at an altitude of 1,000 meters in the Sierra de Cameros, offering pleasant conditions during spring and autumn. Summer can also be a good time, especially given the availability of water sports and swimming areas at the González Lacasa Reservoir, but be mindful of midday heat. Winters may bring colder temperatures and potential snow.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Embalse de González Lacasa, El Rasillo, Peñaloscintos circular route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain generally consists of mostly accessible paths, suitable for jogging. However, some sections may require sure-footedness. You'll encounter natural, unpaved surfaces, passing through pine forests, rebollo oak, and some holm oak, providing varied ground cover and shaded areas.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This 12.5 km circular jogging route is estimated to take approximately 1 hour and 26 minutes to complete, assuming a moderate jogging pace. It requires a good level of fitness due to its moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while jogging around the Embalse de González Lacasa?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful and diverse scenery. The route offers picturesque views of the deep, cold waters of the González Lacasa Reservoir and the surrounding green mountains of the Sierra de Cameros. The natural setting includes pine forests, rebollo oak, and holm oak, creating a serene atmosphere.

faqs.question: Are there other activities available near the Embalse de González Lacasa?
faqs.answer: Yes, the González Lacasa Reservoir is a popular destination for various recreational activities. It features a nautical club offering water sports like sailing, windsurfing, canoeing, and pedal boating. There are also designated swimming and fishing areas, especially during the summer months. The region also provides opportunities for mountaineering and exploring cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Does this jogging trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with other trails. You will find sections that are part of the Sierras Riojanas (40% of the route), Ruta romana del Iregua tramo 3 (14%), Subida a la cueva de La Paz (4%), and Puente de Hierro (4%).

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de González Lacasa, El Rasillo, Peñaloscintos circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This specific 12.5 km route is categorized as 'moderate' for jogging and requires good fitness. While paths are mostly accessible, some sections demand sure-footedness. For beginners or those seeking an easier option, there is an easier 10.1 km circular route around the reservoir that is suitable for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail after jogging?
faqs.answer: The village of El Rasillo de Cameros, located near the reservoir, is likely to offer cafes or establishments where you can refuel after your jog. The nautical club at the reservoir also provides facilities during summer months.
